description: Score tactic n-grams by bridge_score = normalized Shannon entropy of their cross-cluster distribution x rarity (1 - mean within-cluster frequency), excluding all-generic n-grams.
motivation: Find the proof-strategy n-grams that DO span many domains and check whether they carry mathematical content.
formal_results: 15 candidates; the top ones are TFAE scaffolding (tfae_have #-># score 0.420 across 9 clusters; tfae_have x4; tfae_finish) and generic rewriting (rw[@] x3).
results_description: The most cross-domain tactic patterns are domain-agnostic proof scaffolding (TFAE machinery, rewriting), not content-bearing strategies.
conclusions: Shared cross-domain proof strategies are shallow generic moves, so proof STRATEGY does not provide a substantive bridge signal - bridges must be sought in shared PREMISES.
